competitive_position.summary: Zycus is front-runner on fit (CLM, AI, Netsuite integration) but under pricing pressure from Coupa and Zip, who dropped costs significantly and remain in the running as of 25 Jun 2026. Abe requested updated Zycus pricing (list vs CC-specific) to defend internally. Win strategy: deliver updated pricing by 2 Jul with total-cost-of-ownership positioning (3-year SaaS + PS + integration vs Coupa/Zip lower SaaS but higher change cost), anchor EB (Jason Chan) on the $32M ROI case already approved, and make Merlin negotiation agent + clause-search genie the Phase 2 differentiators Coupa/Zip cannot match.

champion_strength.source: Next Step 25 Jun 2026, Contact Role, Task log
champion_strength.summary: Abe Comarow (Senior Strategic Sourcing Manager) is a strong, active champion with direct EB access and clear advocacy (drove $32M business case, coordinated all validation sessions, confirmed 'we are the front runner' 25 Jun). His last contact was 25 Jun (6 days ago), and he is currently driving the updated-pricing request and SOW scoping call. Risk: Abe is under internal pressure to justify Zycus price vs Coupa/Zip (who dropped costs significantly per 25 Jun Next Step). If Zycus does not deliver competitive pricing with ROI positioning by 2 Jul, Abe's ability to champion may weaken. Mitigation: deliver updated pricing 2 Jul, offer direct Abe + Jason Chan (EB) check-in to anchor on total cost of ownership and $32M ROI.

sources: Contact Role: Jason Chan, Controller, Economic Buyer
Next Step 28 Apr 2026: 'CC business case readout with CFO May 8'
Next Step 25 Jun 2026: Abe confirmed 'we are the front runner'
Next Step log: no Jason Chan appearance on recent validation calls (InfoSec, AP, Legal/CLM)
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: Jason Chan (Controller, consumercellular.com) is the Economic Buyer. He received the $32M ROI business case readout on 8 May 2026 (Next Step 28 Apr) and the evaluation progressed post-readout, confirming he controls budget authority. Jason has not appeared on recent calls (InfoSec 8 Jun, AP demo 8 Jun, Legal/CLM 18 Jun per Next Step log), signaling passive sponsorship typical of a finance EB who delegates technical validation to procurement (Abe) and legal (Bobbie Reyes GC). No task or event logs a final written budget sign-off from Jason post-readout, which is a hygiene gap (the approval is implied by continued progression to Shortlisted and legal review, but not documented). Abe confirmed 25 Jun 'we are the front runner', which would not be stated without EB alignment. Jason's last logged contact is the 8 May readout (53 days ago as of 1 Jul 2026). The risk: if Coupa/Zip force a significant price reduction and Zycus cannot match, Jason may re-open budget discussions or delay to Q3/Q4 fiscal planning. Mitigation: the updated pricing Abe requested 25 Jun must land with total-cost-of-ownership positioning (SaaS + PS + integration vs Coupa/Zip lower SaaS but higher change-order cost), and the deal team should request a direct Jason Chan check-in before final signature to confirm budget and timing.

recommended_moves: Deliver updated pricing proposal (list cost vs Consumer Cellular-specific pricing) with total-cost-of-ownership analysis (3-year SaaS + PS + Netsuite integration + Merlin AI vs Coupa/Zip lower SaaS but higher implementation and change-order cost). Tie every line item back to the $32M ROI case CFO Jason Chan approved 8 May. Include competitive benchmark showing Coupa/Zip true landed cost, not just SaaS list price. Position Merlin negotiation agent and clause-search genie as Phase 2 hero features Coupa/Zip cannot match.

Schedule and conduct SOW deep-dive call with Abe by 2 Jul to scope PS roles, resources, and finalize implementation cost estimate. Present fixed-price or capped PS model to de-risk budget overrun. Tie every PS line item to the $32M ROI case and the Spring 2027 Netsuite renewal forcing function.

Request direct check-in with the relevant stakeholder Chan (Controller/CFO) by 5 Jul, led by deal owner's manager, to anchor on total cost of ownership, reinforce the $32M ROI case approved 8 May, and confirm budget and timing ahead of final decision. Position this as a 'pre-signature alignment call' to ensure no surprises when legal approval completes mid-Jul.
